destinations.title: Bimini, Bahamas
destinations.subtitle: Billfish Capital of the World
destinations.teaser: 60 miles east of Miami on the edge of the Gulf Stream. Schoolie dolphin to blue marlin offshore, world-class bonefishing on the flats.

destinations.title: Andros Island, Bahamas
destinations.subtitle: Bonefish Capital of the World
destinations.teaser: The largest island in the Bahamas and the undisputed bonefish capital. Miles of pristine flats with fish averaging 3-5 pounds.

destinations.title: Nassau, Bahamas
destinations.subtitle: Gateway to the Out Islands
destinations.teaser: Easy access from the US with a full-service charter fleet. Offshore billfish grounds within 30 minutes, bonefishing on the western flats.

destinations.title: Grand Bahama Island
destinations.subtitle: Wahoo Alley & Flats Paradise
destinations.teaser: Wahoo off West End, yellowfin and marlin south of Lucaya, world-class bonefishing on the east end. All within a short flight from Florida.

destinations.title: Cat Island, Bahamas
destinations.subtitle: Off the Beaten Path
destinations.teaser: Remote, uncrowded, and packed with fish. Hawk's Nest Resort is your base for offshore billfish and wade fishing on pristine flats.

destinations.title: Long Island, Bahamas
destinations.subtitle: Southern Bahamas Gem
destinations.teaser: Flying Fish Marina at Clarence Town is the southernmost full-service stop in the Bahamas. Great pelagics off the northern tip, bonefishing on the western shore.

destinations.title: Ocean City, Maryland
destinations.subtitle: White Marlin Capital of the World
destinations.teaser: Home of the White Marlin Open — the world's largest billfish tournament. Canyon runs for marlin and tuna, plus inshore rockfish and flounder.

destinations.title: Tahiti, French Polynesia
destinations.subtitle: Where Zane Grey Fished
destinations.teaser: Site of the first 1,000-lb marlin in 1930. Tuamotu atolls and Austral Islands offer some of the most remote blue-water fishing on Earth.

destinations.title: Tropic Star Lodge, Panama
destinations.subtitle: Zane Grey Reef
destinations.teaser: 100 miles from the nearest road in Panama's Darien jungle. Zane Grey Reef sits 5 miles offshore — black marlin, sailfish, and roosterfish in staggering numbers.

destinations.title: Kona, Hawaii
destinations.subtitle: Grander Capital
destinations.teaser: 166 granders and 9 World Cup victories. Deep blue water within a mile of shore — no canyon runs needed. The benchmark for Pacific blue marlin fishing.

destinations.title: Bermuda
destinations.subtitle: Challenger Bank Blue Marlin
destinations.teaser: 8 World Cup wins and 19 granders on the books. Challenger Bank and Argus Bank produce some of the largest blue marlin in the Atlantic.

destinations.title: Outer Banks, North Carolina
destinations.subtitle: Oregon Inlet & Hatteras Bluewater
destinations.teaser: Oregon Inlet and Hatteras give Outer Banks crews fast shots at blue marlin, yellowfin tuna, sailfish, mahi, and wahoo when the Gulf Stream pushes tight to the beach.

destinations.title: Costa Rica
destinations.subtitle: Sailfish Capital of the World
destinations.teaser: Los Sueños and Quepos deliver 20+ sailfish days in peak season. Inshore roosterfish and snapper year-round. Family-friendly infrastructure.

destinations.title: Guatemala
destinations.subtitle: 40+ Sailfish Releases Per Day
destinations.teaser: Iztapa and Puerto Quetzal produce the highest sailfish-per-day averages on the planet. 40+ release days are not uncommon in peak season.

destinations.title: Cabo Blanco, Peru
destinations.subtitle: All-Tackle Black Marlin Record
destinations.teaser: Home of the all-tackle black marlin world record. The Humboldt Current creates a nutrient-rich upwelling that draws massive pelagics close to shore.

destinations.title: Cape Verde Islands
destinations.subtitle: 25+ Grander Blue Marlin
destinations.teaser: 25 blue marlin over 1,000 lbs caught here. Remote volcanic islands off West Africa with some of the purest blue marlin fishing anywhere.

destinations.title: Madeira, Portugal
destinations.subtitle: Atlantic Blue Marlin Hotspot
destinations.teaser: 6 World Cup wins and 58 granders. Volcanic seamounts create upwelling that concentrates blue marlin within 3 miles of shore.

destinations.title: Cairns, Australia
destinations.subtitle: Black Marlin Mecca
destinations.teaser: 894 black marlin over 1,000 lbs caught along the Great Barrier Reef. The September-December season draws serious anglers from around the world.

destinations.title: St. Thomas, USVI
destinations.subtitle: The North Drop
destinations.teaser: The North Drop — a dramatic ledge where the ocean floor falls from 600 to 6,000 feet. Blue marlin, yellowfin tuna, and wahoo stack up on the edge.

destinations.title: Venice, Louisiana
destinations.subtitle: Tuna Town USA
destinations.teaser: Where the Mississippi meets the Gulf. Oil rigs create structure that holds yellowfin tuna, and the deep blue is loaded with marlin and mahi.

destinations.title: Isla Mujeres, Mexico
destinations.subtitle: Sailfish Sardine Run
destinations.teaser: The winter sardine run pulls massive schools of sailfish within sight of the island. Packs of 50+ sailfish balling bait — a bucket-list spectacle.

destinations.title: Seychelles
destinations.subtitle: Indian Ocean Gamefish Paradise
destinations.teaser: Blue and black marlin, giant trevally on fly, dogtooth tuna on the reef. Some of the most pristine blue water left on the planet.

destinations.title: New Jersey Shore
destinations.subtitle: Monster Spring Stripers by Kayak
destinations.teaser: Raritan Bay to Cape May — New Jersey's striper run produces 40+ lb fish from a kayak. Clam bellies and bunker chunks from April through June.

destinations.title: Florida Keys
destinations.subtitle: Keys Sailfish, Tarpon & Flats Grand Slam
destinations.teaser: Islamorada to Key West is pure Florida Keys fishing: winter sailfish on the reef edge, spring tarpon under the bridges, and year-round bonefish and permit on the flats.

destinations.title: Dominican Republic
destinations.subtitle: Cap Cana Blue Marlin
destinations.teaser: Cap Cana and Punta Cana sit on the edge of the Mona Passage — one of the most productive blue marlin corridors in the Caribbean.

destinations.title: Exmouth, Western Australia
destinations.subtitle: Ningaloo Blue Marlin
destinations.teaser: First Australian blue marlin over 1,000 lbs caught here in 2018. Ningaloo Reef drops to 1,000m within a mile — deep water and big fish, right off the beach.

destinations.title: The Azores, Portugal
destinations.subtitle: Condor Bank Giants
destinations.teaser: 14 blue marlin over 1,000 lbs on Condor Bank. Mid-Atlantic volcanic seamounts create perfect conditions for monster blues and bigeye tuna.

species_list.label: Blue Marlin
species_list.description: Big-game bluewater target with top destinations for blue and black marlin shots.

species_list.label: Sailfish
species_list.description: Fast-action sailfish fisheries, from baitball chaos to high-release charter programs.

species_list.label: Tuna
species_list.description: Yellowfin-leaning tuna water with proven offshore runs and structure.

species_list.label: Mahi-Mahi
species_list.description: High-speed dolphin action around current lines, weed edges, and bluewater structure.

species_list.label: Wahoo
species_list.description: High-speed wahoo destinations where temperature breaks and ledges matter.

species_list.label: Bonefish
species_list.description: Flats-driven destinations for sight-fishing, wading, and technical presentations.

species_list.label: Tarpon
species_list.description: Big migratory silver kings and warm-water inshore ambush lanes.

species_list.label: Giant Trevally
species_list.description: Heavy-tackle giant trevally opportunities around reefs, drops, and oceanic islands.

species_list.label: Roosterfish
species_list.description: Pacific inshore bruisers with beach, rock, and panga credibility.

species_list.label: Snapper
species_list.description: Reef and structure-oriented snapper fisheries that overlap bluewater programs.

species_list.label: Striped Bass
species_list.description: Mid-Atlantic and Northeast migratory bass water.

species_list.label: Bluefish
species_list.description: Aggressive nearshore surface-feeding action, mostly in US East overlap zones.

regions.label: Bahamas
regions.description: Bimini to Long Island — bluewater icons, skinny-water bonefish, and easy island-hopping charter access.
regions.hero: Start in the Bahamas for the cleanest mix of offshore destinations and flats targets in the app.

regions.label: Caribbean
regions.description: Island drops, sailfish baitballs, and big-water pelagics across the Caribbean basin.
regions.hero: Caribbean regions blend classic bluewater runs with quick-hit resort access.

regions.label: Mid-Atlantic Canyons & Striper Coast
regions.description: White marlin, tuna, striped bass, and bluefish water from New Jersey through Ocean City — offshore canyon shots plus nearshore migration lanes.
regions.hero: This zone matches how East Coast anglers actually talk: canyon trolling when the weather lets you run, striped-bass and bluefish opportunities when you stay closer to the beach.

regions.label: Outer Banks, NC
regions.description: Oregon Inlet, Hatteras, and classic OBX bluewater with blue marlin, yellowfin tuna, sailfish, mahi, and wahoo in the mix.
regions.hero: This is the clean SEO version of the fishery Tim actually wants to surface: Outer Banks charter water, not a vague Carolina blob. Think Oregon Inlet runs, Hatteras edge bites, and billfish-heavy summer shots.

regions.label: Carolina Gulf Stream
regions.description: Bluewater marlin, sailfish, tuna, mahi, and wahoo along the Carolina offshore corridor where ledges, break lines, and Gulf Stream positioning drive the bite.
regions.hero: Yes, this overlaps the Outer Banks in real life. That is the point here: SEO and buyer intent. Treat this as the broader Carolina bluewater lane without pretending the map already has broad pin coverage up and down the coast.

regions.label: South Florida Sailfish Coast
regions.description: Palm Beach, Fort Lauderdale, Miami, and Treasure Coast style Atlantic-side fishing built around winter sailfish, mahi, wahoo, and fast bluewater access.
regions.hero: This is the Florida Atlantic-side fishery anglers actually search for: sailfish alley, reef-edge kite fishing, and quick runs to blue water. Coverage is intentionally narrow for now rather than faking a bunch of pins.

regions.label: Florida Keys
regions.description: Islamorada to Key West with reef-edge sailfish, bridge tarpon, flats bonefish, permit, and classic mixed-bag Keys access.
regions.hero: Keep the Keys separate. It is its own fishery, its own search intent, and its own style of trip: reef edge outside, backcountry and flats inside, tarpon bridges when the migration lights up.

regions.label: US Gulf Coast
regions.description: Oil-rig structure, tuna-heavy offshore runs, and Gulf pelagic consistency.
regions.hero: The Gulf is a compact but lethal yellowfin-and-marlin region centered on proven structure.

regions.label: Central America
regions.description: High-volume Pacific sailfish, roosterfish coastlines, and jungle-lodge marlin programs.
regions.hero: Central America is where release numbers get silly and inshore roosterfish stay in play.

regions.label: South America
regions.description: Historic record-class marlin water and raw Pacific upwelling zones.
regions.hero: Small on pin count for now, but this region carries serious heavyweight history.

regions.label: Atlantic Islands
regions.description: Remote oceanic islands with volcanic relief, big blue marlin, and long-range Atlantic pedigree.
regions.hero: Atlantic islands are for anglers chasing classic big-fish water, not convenience.

regions.label: Pacific
regions.description: Legendary marlin grounds stretching from Hawaii to French Polynesia to Australia.
regions.hero: The Pacific region is the blue-marlin fantasy draft.

regions.label: Indian Ocean
regions.description: Remote current edges, GT-ready structure, and big-water pelagic variety.
regions.hero: Indian Ocean coverage starts with Seychelles — remote, technical, and worth the flight.
